preview_text:
The model favors Bosnia and Herzegovina at 46%, but grants Qatar a meaningful 23% chance to win outright. The sportsbooks are considerably more bullish on the underdog's opponent, pricing a Bosnia win at 66%—a gap of nearly 20 percentage points. Polymarket prices align closely with sportsbook consensus, both rating Qatar's upset probability at roughly 13%.

The model sees this as a competitive match with draw odds at 31%, suggesting neither side dominates decisively in its baseline projection. The win probabilities for both teams remain substantive, indicating genuine uncertainty about the outcome. A draw is plausible under the model's framing, though less likely than either team winning.

The gap between model and market is substantial and consistent across sportsbooks and Polymarket. The model assigns Bosnia a notably lower win probability and Qatar a notably higher one than consensus. The dashboard makes no claim to resolve which assessment is correct; the divergence reflects different weighting of available information or risk aversion in betting markets.
